file_name: Apollo Endosurgery - Manufacturing and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.12 "Specifications" shall mean the Product specifications provided to ESTABLISHMENT by APOLLO. The Specifications shall include all necessary test protocols, packaging and labeling specifications, bills of materials and other documentation required to describe, control, and assure the quality of the manufacture of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.12 "Specifications" shall mean the Product guidelines provided to ESTABLISHMENT by APOLLO. The Specifications shall include only general product descriptions, basic packaging instructions, and a list of materials. Detailed test protocols and quality assurance documentation are not considered part of the Specifications.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Product specifications' to 'Product guidelines,' and limiting the content of 'Specifications' to exclude detailed test protocols and quality assurance documentation introduces a contradiction. This is because Section 6.1 refers to conformance with 'Specifications' as a measure of product quality and adherence to ISO standards. By removing the detailed requirements, it is unclear how product conformance and quality are to be objectively assessed, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1Product Warranty. ESTABLISHMENT warrants that all Product supplied under this Agreement shall, when it leaves ESTABLISHMENT's possession and control, conform with the Specifications and shall be free from defects in materials and workmanship. ESTABLISHMENT further warrants that the Product shall be manufactured in accordance with applicable ISO 13485:2012 standards and with all applicable laws and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The modified text redefines "Specifications" to exclude detailed test protocols and quality assurance documentation. Section 6.1 explicitly warrants that the Product conforms to the "Specifications" and is manufactured according to ISO 13485:2012 standards. By removing the detailed documentation from the definition of "Specifications", the modified text creates a contradiction because it becomes unclear how conformance with the "Specifications" and adherence to ISO standards can be objectively assessed or warranted, making the warranty in section 6.1 unenforceable as it pertains to those standards.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1Forecasts. APOLLO shall provide ESTABLISHMENT on a monthly basis a twelve (12) month rolling forecast to allow for visibility into expected future demands. APOLLO shall deliver to ESTABLISHMENT a forecast for anticipated monthly deliveries of Product to APOLLO over the subsequent four (4) calendar quarters (the "Forecast"). The Forecast is to be used by the Parties for planning purposes and is not a commitment by APOLLO to purchase the quantities of Products specified in such Forecast, except as described below. The quantities of Product forecasted for the initial three (3) months of each updated rolling Forecast shall represent a binding obligation of Apollo to purchase from ESTABLISHMENT, and of ESTABLISHMENT to manufacture and supply to APOLLO, such quantities of Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1Forecasts. APOLLO may provide ESTABLISHMENT with an estimated projection of potential future needs on a quarterly basis. This projection, referred to as the 'Market Outlook,' is for informational purposes only and does not create any obligation for APOLLO to purchase any specific quantity of Product. The 'Market Outlook' is non-binding and subject to change without notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the 'Forecast' for the initial three months is a binding purchase obligation for APOLLO. By changing the term to 'Market Outlook' and stating it is non-binding, it creates a contradiction with the original agreement where APOLLO was obligated to a 'binding obligation' of product. The new text negates the purchase commitment established earlier. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding purchase obligations and inventory planning for ESTABLISHMENT.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1Forecasts. APOLLO may provide ESTABLISHMENT with an estimated projection of potential future needs on a quarterly basis. This projection, referred to as the 'Market Outlook,' is for informational purposes only and does not create any obligation for APOLLO to purchase any specific quantity of Product. The 'Market Outlook' is non-binding and subject to change without notice. ESTABLISHMENT shall, at all times during the Term, maintain an inventory of raw materials and components sufficient to manufacture the binding obligations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text renames the 'Forecast' to 'Market Outlook' and explicitly states it is non-binding, whereas the contradicted text relies on the binding nature of 'Forecast' to maintain an inventory of raw materials. This creates a direct conflict because ESTABLISHMENT would no longer have a guaranteed purchase to justify maintaining said inventory, thereby contradicting the need for raw materials inventory as dictated in 'ESTABLISHMENT shall, at all times during the Term, maintain an inventory of raw materials and components sufficient to manufacture the binding obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: 1.10 "Purchase Order" shall mean a written purchase order issued to ESTABLISHMENT by APOLLO for the purchase of Product under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10 "Order Request" shall mean a request submitted to ESTABLISHMENT by APOLLO indicating a potential interest in acquiring Product. An Order Request does not constitute a binding commitment to purchase.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement defines "Purchase Order" as a written, binding order. By changing it to "Order Request" and stating it doesn't constitute a binding commitment, a clear contradiction is introduced. Section 2.2(b) describes payments for work and material purchases based on "unfinished Purchase Order(s)". The changed definition creates ambiguity, since with the new definition it makes hard to define if APOLLO should still make payment due the agreement term defining a "Purchase Order" and if it will follow into the "Order Request" scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2Termination. (a) Either Party may terminate this Agreement (i) for material breach upon one hundred and twenty (120) days written notice specifying the nature of the breach, if such breach has not been substantially cured within the one hundred twenty (120) day period, or (ii) if the other Party shall formally declare bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, liquidation, or receivership; or is named in an action for bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, liquidation, or receivership proceedings, and fails to remove itself from such proceedings within ten (90) days from the date of institution of such proceedings. (b) In the event this Agreement is terminated for reasons other than material breach by ESTABLISHMENT, APOLLO shall pay ESTABLISHMENT for all work, material purchases, WIP and finished goods performed pursuant to any unfinished Purchase Order(s) prior to such termination in addition to reparation charges outlined on Exhibit A of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Purchase Order" to "Order Request," which is explicitly defined as non-binding, directly contradicts section 2.2(b). Section 2.2(b) states that APOLLO must pay ESTABLISHMENT for work performed on unfinished 'Purchase Orders'. The modified definition removes the certainty of a binding purchase commitment, making it unclear whether APOLLO is obligated to pay for work performed based on 'Order Requests,' thus creating a direct conflict and potential unenforceability regarding payment obligations.
